papers.description: The assessment has a gradient of difficulty throughout the paper and consists of a mix of short and long questions.
Structured in two sections of approximately 50 marks each: pure mathematics and statistics. Some questions will be set on the pre-release data set.
Structured in two sections of approximately 50 marks each: pure mathematics and mechanics.

topics.name: Proof
subtopics: Understand and use the structure of mathematical proof
Proof by deduction
Proof by exhaustion
Disproof by counterexample
Proof by contradiction (including proof of the irrationality of sqrt(2) and the infinity of primes)

topics.name: Algebra and Functions
subtopics: Understand and use the laws of indices for all rational exponents
Use and manipulate surds, including rationalising the denominator
Work with quadratic functions and their graphs
Solve quadratic equations including completing the square and using the quadratic formula
Solve simultaneous equations in two variables by elimination and substitution
Solve linear and quadratic inequalities
Manipulate polynomials algebraically
Understand and use graphs of functions
Understand the effect of simple transformations on graphs
Use of functions in modelling

topics.name: Coordinate Geometry in the (x, y) Plane
subtopics: Understand and use the equation of a straight line
Use the form y = mx + c, ax + by + c = 0
Understand the equation of a circle
Find the centre and radius of a circle from its equation
Use the properties of a circle to solve geometric problems
Find equations of tangents and normals to circles

topics.name: Sequences and Series
subtopics: Understand and use the binomial expansion for positive integer n
Work with sequences including those given by a formula for the nth term
Understand and use sigma notation for sums of series
Understand and work with arithmetic sequences and series
Understand and work with geometric sequences and series
Understand the convergence condition for infinite geometric series

topics.name: Trigonometry
subtopics: Understand and use the definitions of sine, cosine and tangent for all arguments
Understand and use the sine and cosine rules
Calculate the area of a triangle using 1/2 ab sin C
Understand and use the sine, cosine and tangent functions and their graphs
Understand and use tan theta = sin theta / cos theta
Understand and use sin^2 theta + cos^2 theta = 1
Solve simple trigonometric equations
Use standard trigonometric identities to solve equations
Understand and use double angle formulae

topics.name: Exponentials and Logarithms
subtopics: Know and use the function a^x and its graph
Know and use the function e^x and its graph
Know that the gradient of e^(kx) is ke^(kx)
Know and use the definition of log_a x as the inverse of a^x
Know and use the function ln x and its graph
Understand and use the laws of logarithms
Use logarithms to solve equations of the form a^x = b
Use exponential growth and decay in modelling

topics.name: Differentiation
subtopics: Understand and use the derivative of f(x) as the gradient of the tangent
Differentiate x^n for rational n
Differentiate e^(kx), ln x, sin kx, cos kx, tan kx
Use the product rule, quotient rule and chain rule
Differentiate using implicit and parametric differentiation
Construct simple differential equations
Apply differentiation to find gradients, tangents and normals
Apply differentiation to maxima and minima and stationary points
Identify where functions are increasing or decreasing

topics.name: Integration
subtopics: Know and use the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
Integrate x^n (excluding n = -1), e^(kx), 1/x, sin kx, cos kx
Use integration by substitution and integration by parts
Use partial fractions to integrate rational functions
Evaluate definite integrals
Use integration to find areas under curves and between curves
Interpret solutions of first order differential equations

topics.name: Numerical Methods
subtopics: Locate roots of f(x) = 0 by considering changes of sign
Understand the use of iteration to solve equations
Use Newton-Raphson method and other iterative methods
Use numerical integration including the trapezium rule
Understand the order of convergence of iterative methods

topics.name: Vectors
subtopics: Use vectors in two dimensions
Calculate the magnitude and direction of a vector
Convert between component form and magnitude/direction form
Add vectors diagrammatically and perform algebraic operations
Understand and use position vectors
Use vectors to solve problems in pure mathematics and mechanics

topics.name: Statistical Sampling
subtopics: Understand and use the terms 'population' and 'sample'
Use samples to make informal inferences about the population
Understand and use sampling techniques including simple random, systematic, stratified and quota sampling
Select or critique sampling techniques in the context of solving a statistical problem

topics.name: Data Presentation and Interpretation
subtopics: Interpret diagrams for single-variable data including choropleth maps
Interpret scatter diagrams and regression lines for bivariate data
Recognise and interpret possible outliers in data sets
Select or critique data presentation techniques
Be able to clean data including dealing with missing data and errors

topics.name: Probability
subtopics: Understand and use mutually exclusive and independent events
Use and understand tree diagrams, Venn diagrams and two-way tables
Understand and use conditional probability
Calculate and interpret conditional probabilities

topics.name: Statistical Distributions
subtopics: Understand and use simple discrete distributions (calculation of expectation and variance)
Understand and use the binomial distribution
Understand and use the normal distribution
Use the normal distribution as a model for continuous data
Select an appropriate probability distribution for a context

topics.name: Statistical Hypothesis Testing
subtopics: Understand and apply the language of statistical hypothesis testing
Conduct statistical hypothesis tests using the binomial distribution
Understand and use the terms null hypothesis, alternative hypothesis, significance level, test statistic, critical region, critical value and p-value
Interpret results in context

topics.name: Quantities and Units in Mechanics
subtopics: Understand and use fundamental quantities and units in the S.I. system
Understand and use derived quantities and units
Understand the difference between scalar and vector quantities

topics.name: Kinematics
subtopics: Understand and use the language of kinematics: position, displacement, distance travelled, speed, velocity, acceleration
Understand and use graphs for kinematics (displacement-time, velocity-time, acceleration-time)
Use calculus in kinematics for motion in a straight line
Understand and use the constant acceleration formulae (suvat equations)
Use vectors to solve kinematics problems in 2D

topics.name: Forces and Newton's Laws
subtopics: Understand the concept of a force
Understand and use Newton's first law
Understand and use Newton's second law for motion in a straight line (F=ma)
Understand and use Newton's third law
Understand and use the weight of a body (W=mg)
Understand and use connected particle problems
Solve problems involving simple pulley systems

topics.name: Moments
subtopics: Understand and use the concept of a moment
Use the principle that a system is in equilibrium when the sum of moments equals zero
Solve simple problems involving equilibrium of a rigid body
